summary: Do not ship yet: R-48 is fixed in code and the current 118/118 regression plus 63/63 characterization suites pass, but the committed GREEN lock still certifies the pre-R-48 implementation containing automatic PR closure.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Release proof still pins the R-48-broken implementation
findings.body: The GREEN SHA remains `c389276`, an ancestor of `de739bb` whose executor still contains `CLOSE_MAX`, `BUMP_PR_CLOSE`, and `gh pr close`. The final executor and regression tests both changed afterward. Consequently, the committed same-test RED→GREEN proof ends at the implementation rejected by R-48; `verification.md:4-9` also calls this stale SHA HEAD. Fresh HEAD tests establish current behavior, but they do not restore the composite gate's machine-owned proof that the final test contract fails on the pre-fix source and passes on the final source.
findings.recommendation: Reconstruct the RED baseline with the exact current 118-test tree, generate a GREEN record for `de739bb` or the final HEAD, repin `green.sha`, and update `verification.md` before rerunning the release gate.

next_steps: Regenerate and commit the final RED/GREEN lock against the post-R-48 production and test trees.
